Package org.jlab.epsci.ersap.engine

Class EngineData

java.lang.Object
org.jlab.epsci.ersap.engine.EngineData
public class EngineData extends Object
Engine data passed in/out to the service engine.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    EngineData()
    Creates an empty engine data object.

    Gets the description of the data and/or status.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • EngineData

      public EngineData()
      Creates an empty engine data object. The user-data must be set with setData(java.lang.String).

  • Method Details

    • getData

      public Object getData()
      Gets the user-data. The value must be cast to its proper Java class. Use getMimeType() to get information about the type of the data.
      Returns:
      the user-data or null if not set

    • getMimeType

      public String getMimeType()
      Gets the mime-type string for the user-data. The mime-type acts as a clue for which Java class must be used when casting the user-data.
      Returns:
      a string with the mime-type or empty if not set

    • setData

      public void setData(String data)
      Sets a new string data for this object.
      Parameters:
      data - the string with the user-data

    • setData

      public void setData(EngineDataType dataType, Object data)
      Sets a new user-data for this object.

      The Java class of the user-data must correspond to the given dataType, which must also be supported by the orchestrator or engine, to serialize the data if necessary.

      Parameters:
      dataType - the ERSAP data-type for the user-data
      data - the object with the user-data

    • setData

      public void setData(String mimeType, Object data)
      Sets a new user-data for this object.

      The mime-type string and the Java class of the user-data must correspond to an existing EngineDataType supported by the orchestrator or engine, to serialize the data if necessary.

      Parameters:
      mimeType - the mime-type for the user-data
      data - the object with the user-data

    • getDescription

      public String getDescription()
      Gets the description of the data and/or status. Each engine can set a description to provide extra information about the result of a request.
      Returns:
      a string with the description or empty if not set

    • setDescription

      public void setDescription(String description)
      Sets a description for the data. It can provide further details about the user-data, or the cause for the error status, etc.
      Parameters:
      description - a description for the data

    • getStatus

      public EngineStatus getStatus()
      Gets the status for the data. Useful to check if the result of an engine execution was a warning or an error.

      The default status is always info.

      Returns:
      the status of the data

    • getStatusSeverity

      public int getStatusSeverity()
      Gets the optional severity for the status of the data.

      The default severity for a new status is 1.

      Returns:
      the value of the severity (engine-specific)

    • setStatus

      public void setStatus(EngineStatus status)
      Sets a new status for this data. Useful to set the result of an engine execution request as a warning or error.
      Parameters:
      status - the new status

    • setStatus

      public void setStatus(EngineStatus status, int severity)
      Sets a new status for this data, with custom severity. Useful to set the result of an engine execution request as a warning or error.

      Each engine defines the interpretation of the severity values.

      Parameters:
      status - the new status
      severity - the custom severity as a positive integer

    • getEngineName

      public String getEngineName()
      Gets the canonical name of the engine that returned this data, if any.

      This value will be set only when the data is the result of a engine request, and it can be obtained by the next service in a composition or by the monitoring orchestrators.

      Returns:
      the canonical name of the engine or empty if not created by an engine

    • getEngineVersion

      public String getEngineVersion()
      Gets the version of the engine that returned this data, if any.

      This value will be set only when the data is the result of a engine request, and it can be obtained by the next service in a composition or by the monitoring orchestrators.

      Returns:
      the version of the engine or empty if not created by an engine

    • getCommunicationId

      public int getCommunicationId()
      Gets the ID for the request this data is part of.

      This value should be set by the orchestrator or data-source engine sending a new request (a single request to an engine, or the first request of a new application composition request).

      Returns:
      the ID of the request

    • setCommunicationId

      public void setCommunicationId(int value)
      Sets an ID for the request this data is part of.

      Orchestrators and data-source engines should set a proper communication ID to identify unique requests. Engines should avoid changing the communication ID in the middle of an application composition request.

      Parameters:
      value - the communication ID

    • getComposition

      public String getComposition()
      Gets the composition for the request this data is part of.

      The composition is set when an orchestrator publishes the data, i.e., only the engines receiving the data (or the orchestrators receiving the results) can observe its value.

      Returns:
      a string with the composition

    • getExecutionState

      public String getExecutionState()
      Gets the state of the execution result set by the engine, if any.

      This value will be set only when the data is the result of a engine, and it is used in composition requests to route the data to the next service in the composition.

      Returns:
      the state set by the engine or empty

    • setExecutionState

      public void setExecutionState(String state)
      Sets an execution state for this data. Engines should set a state for the result of processing a specific request, and it should be one of the defined states by the engine.

      States define the flow the output data in a composition request, where results are routed to the next services based on its state.

      Parameters:
      state - the state for the data

    • getExecutionTime

      public long getExecutionTime()
      Gets the time that took the engine to process a request and return this data. If set, this is the time spent by a successful request to the configure or execute method of the engine that created this data.

      This value will be set only when the data is the result of a engine request, and it can be obtained by the next service in a composition or by the monitoring orchestrators.

      Returns:
      the execution time of the request or 0 if not a result of a request

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
